title: OWASP Top 10
answer: OWASP Top 10 is a practical list of major web application security risks like broken access control, injection, insecure design, and security misconfiguration.
bullets: Use it as a prioritization guide, not a full security standard.
Map each risk to prevention, detection, and validation steps.
Start with access control, injection, and secrets handling for fast impact.

title: Phishing Triage Checklist
answer: For phishing triage, first preserve the message, inspect sender/domain indicators, review links and attachments safely, and decide on containment before broad communication.
bullets: Do not click links or open attachments on the primary workstation.
Check sender spoofing, reply-to mismatch, and urgent language.
Contain impacted accounts, then collect indicators for blocking and awareness.

title: Secure Coding Checklist
answer: A solid secure coding checklist covers input validation, authentication and authorization, secret handling, output encoding, dependency hygiene, and logging without leaking sensitive data.
bullets: Validate and normalize untrusted input at system boundaries.
Use least privilege for data access and background jobs.
Keep dependencies patched and remove dead packages.
